### Post by sreejithemk on 2008-07-13
I am using Ubuntu 8.04 and recently I'm not able to automount my CDROMS, and USB pendrives. My /etc/fstab is like
```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.

#

#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 

#

# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>



proc /proc proc defaults 0 0

# Entry for /dev/sda3 :

UUID=6e35d56a-9067-47d8-b84e-50ca84a2ff89 / ext3 relatime,errors=remount-ro 0 1

# Entry for /dev/sda8 :

UUID=b27e569b-036b-4d6b-916e-563924cdc3bf none swap sw 0 0

/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0

/dev/sda7 /media/Misc ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_IN 0 0

/dev/sda6 /media/Movies ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_IN 0 0

/dev/sda5 /media/Games ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_IN 0 0

/dev/sda1 /media/WinXP ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_IN 0 0


```

Please help me to regain the automount

---

### Post by sreejithemk on 2008-07-31
I didn't get anything yet. Can anyone help please..........

The drives are actually shown in the 'Computer' when I connect them. But they are not mounted.
Now I'm using the following comand to mount them..........

gnome-mount -vbd /dev/scd0 (For CD/DVD)
gnome-mount -vbd /dev/sdb1 (For PenDrive)

Anyone got other ideas................?
